title: AWS Intro
content:
Amazon Web Services Introduction

The World's Leading Cloud

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world's most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform, offering over 200 fully featured services from data centers globally.



  • AWS provides on-demand delivery of IT resources over the internet with pay-as-you-go pricing.
  • Millions of customers—including the fastest-growing startups, largest enterprises, and leading government agencies—are using AWS to lower costs, become more agile, and innovate faster.
  • AWS launched in 2006 and has been the leader in cloud computing ever since.

title: AWS Infrastructure
content:
AWS Global Infrastructure

Regions and Availability Zones

AWS has the concept of a Region, which is a physical location around the world where we cluster data centers. Each AWS Region consists of multiple, isolated, and physically separate AZs within a geographic area.



  • Regions: Geographic areas that host multiple Availability Zones. Examples: us-east-1 (N. Virginia), eu-west-1 (Ireland).
  • Availability Zones (AZs): One or more discrete data centers with redundant power, networking, and connectivity in an AWS Region.
  • Edge Locations: Points of Presence used by services like Amazon CloudFront to deliver content to users with low latency.
Describing Regions (AWS CLI)
# List all available AWS Regions
aws ec2 describe-regions --query "Regions[].RegionName" --output table

title: Compute (EC2)
content:
Amazon EC2

Virtual Servers in the Cloud

Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) provides scalable computing capacity in the AWS Cloud. Using Amazon EC2 eliminates your need to invest in hardware up front, so you can develop and deploy applications faster.

Launching an Instance (AWS CLI)
# Launch a t2.micro instance using the AWS CLI
aws ec2 run-instances \
    --image-id ami-xxxxxxxx \
    --count 1 \
    --instance-type t2.micro \
    --key-name MyKeyPair \
    --security-group-ids sg-xxxxxxxx

title: Storage (S3)
content:
Amazon S3

Object Storage built to retrieve any amount of data from anywhere

Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is an object storage service that offers industry-leading scalability, data availability, security, and performance. You can use it to store and protect any amount of data for a range of use cases.



  • Buckets: Containers for objects stored in Amazon S3. Names must be globally unique.
  • Objects: The fundamental entities stored in Amazon S3 (files).
  • Storage Classes: S3 Standard, S3 Intelligent-Tiering, S3 Standard-IA, S3 One Zone-IA, S3 Glacier, etc.
Uploading a file (Python Boto3)
import boto3

# Create an S3 client
s3 = boto3.client('s3')

# Upload a file to a bucket
s3.upload_file('local_file.txt', 'my-unique-bucket-name', 'remote_file.txt')

print("Upload Successful")

title: Database (RDS)
content:
Amazon RDS

Managed Relational Database Service

Amazon Relational Database Service (Amazon RDS) makes it easy to set up, operate, and scale a relational database in the cloud. It provides cost-efficient and resizable capacity while automating time-consuming administration tasks.



  • Supported engines: MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and Amazon Aurora.
  • Automated patches, backups, and software updates.
  • Multi-AZ deployment for high availability.
Creating a DB Instance (AWS CLI)
# Create a MySQL database instance
aws rds create-db-instance \
    --db-instance-identifier mydbinstance \
    --db-instance-class db.t3.micro \
    --engine mysql \
    --allocated-storage 20 \
    --master-username admin \
    --master-user-password secretpassword

title: Serverless (Lambda)
content:
AWS Lambda

Run Code without Thinking about Servers

AWS Lambda is a serverless, event-driven compute service that lets you run code for virtually any type of application or backend service without provisioning or managing servers.

Lambda Function (Node.js)
export const handler = async (event) => {
    const name = event.name || "World";
    const response = {
        statusCode: 200,
        body: JSON.stringify(`Hello from Lambda, ${name}!`),
    };
    return response;
};

title: Cloud Types
content:
IaaS, PaaS, SaaS

Cloud Computing Service Models

Cloud computing is often categorized into three main service models, each representing a different part of the cloud computing stack.



  • Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS): Provides basic building blocks for cloud IT (networking, computers, data storage). Example: Amazon EC2.
  • Platform as a Service (PaaS): Removes the need for you to manage underlying infrastructure (hardware/OS). Example: AWS Elastic Beanstalk.
  • Software as a Service (SaaS): A completed product that is run and managed by the service provider. Example: Salesforce, Dropbox, Gmail.

title: AWS Free Tier
content:
AWS Free Tier

Get Started for Free

AWS Free Tier provides customers the ability to explore and try out AWS services free of charge up to specified limits for each service.



  • Always Free: These free tier offers do not expire and are available to all AWS customers. Example: AWS Lambda (1M free requests per month).
  • 12 Months Free: Enjoy these offers for 12 months following your initial sign-up date to AWS. Example: Amazon EC2 (750 hours per month).
  • Trials: Short-term free trial offers starting from the date you activate a particular service.

title: Networking (VPC)
content:
Amazon VPC

Your Private Cloud in AWS

Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (Amazon VPC) lets you provision a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network that you define.



  • Subnets: A range of IP addresses in your VPC. Public subnets have a route to an Internet Gateway; private subnets do not.
  • Internet Gateway (IGW): Allows communication between your VPC and the internet.
  • Security Groups: Virtual firewalls for your EC2 instances to control incoming and outgoing traffic.
  • Network ACLs: Optional layer of security for your VPC that acts as a firewall for controlling traffic in and out of one or more subnets.
Creating a VPC (AWS CLI)
# Create a VPC with a specific CIDR block
aws ec2 create-vpc --cidr-block 10.0.0.0/16

title: Security (IAM)
content:
AWS IAM

Manage Access to AWS Resources

AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) enables you to manage access to AWS services and resources securely. With IAM, you can create and manage AWS users and groups, and use permissions to allow and deny their access to AWS resources.



  • Users: Individuals who interact with AWS.
  • Groups: Collections of users who have the same permissions.
  • Roles: Identities that can be assumed by users, applications, or services.
  • Policies: JSON documents that define permissions.
IAM Policy Example (JSON)
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": "s3:ListBucket",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::example_bucket"
    }
  ]
}

title: NoSQL (DynamoDB)
content:
Amazon DynamoDB

Fast and Flexible NoSQL Database

Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service that provides fast and predictable performance with seamless scalability. It's a key-value and document database that delivers single-digit millisecond performance at any scale.



  • Fully managed and serverless — no infrastructure to manage.
  • Automatically scales up and down to adjust for capacity and maintain performance.
  • Built-in security, backup and restore, and in-memory caching.
Creating a Table (AWS CLI)
# Create a DynamoDB table with a primary key
aws dynamodb create-table \
    --table-name Music \
    --attribute-definitions AttributeName=Artist,AttributeType=S \
    --key-schema AttributeName=Artist,KeyType=HASH \
    --provisioned-throughput ReadCapacityUnits=5,WriteCapacityUnits=5

title: Load Balancing (ELB)
content:
Elastic Load Balancing

Distribute Traffic Across Targets

Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets and virtual appliances in one or more Availability Zones.



  • Application Load Balancer (ALB): Decides based on HTTP/HTTPS traffic (Layer 7).
  • Network Load Balancer (NLB): Best for TCP/UDP traffic requiring ultra-high performance (Layer 4).
  • Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB): Used for deploying and managing third-party virtual appliances.
Creating an ALB (AWS CLI)
# Create an Application Load Balancer
aws elbv2 create-load-balancer \
    --name my-load-balancer \
    --subnets subnet-xxxxxxxx subnet-yyyyyyyy \
    --security-groups sg-xxxxxxxx

title: Auto Scaling (ASG)
content:
Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling

Maintain Instance Availability

Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling helps you ensure that you have the correct number of Amazon EC2 instances available to handle the load for your application.



  • Scaling Policies: Dynamic scaling (respond to demand) and predictive scaling (predict demand).
  • Health Checks: Automatically replaces impaired instances.
  • Fleet Management: Distributes instances across Availability Zones for high availability.
Creating an Auto Scaling Group (AWS CLI)
# Create an Auto Scaling Group
aws autoscaling create-auto-scaling-group \
    --auto-scaling-group-name my-asg \
    --launch-template LaunchTemplateId=lt-xxxxxxxx \
    --min-size 1 --max-size 3 --desired-capacity 2 \
    --vpc-zone-identifier "subnet-xxxxxxxx,subnet-yyyyyyyy"

title: Monitoring (CloudWatch)
content:
Amazon CloudWatch

Monitor Resources and Applications

Amazon CloudWatch is a monitoring and observability service built for DevOps engineers, developers, site reliability engineers (SREs), and IT managers.



  • Metrics: Collect and track performance data for AWS resources.
  • Logs: Monitor, store, and access your log files from EC2, Lambda, and more.
  • Alarms: Send notifications or automatically make changes to the resources you are monitoring based on rules.
  • Dashboards: Create reusable graphs and visualizations.
Creating a CloudWatch Alarm (AWS CLI)
# Create an alarm for CPU usage
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name cpu-mon \
    --metric-name CPUUtilization \
    --namespace AWS/EC2 \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --threshold 70 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --dimensions Name=InstanceId,Value=i-xxxxxxxx \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:my-topic

title: CloudFront (CDN)
content:
Amazon CloudFront

Global Content Delivery Network

Amazon CloudFront is a fast content delivery network (CDN) service that securely delivers data, videos, applications, and APIs to customers globally with low latency and high transfer speeds.



  • Edge Locations: Points of Presence around the world where content is cached.
  • HTTPS Integration: Securely deliver content using SSL/TLS.
  • Integration: Works seamlessly with S3, EC2, ELB, and Lambda@Edge.
Creating a Distribution (AWS CLI)
# Create a CloudFront distribution for an S3 bucket
aws cloudfront create-distribution \
    --origin-domain-name my-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com

title: IaC (CloudFormation)
content:
AWS CloudFormation

Infrastructure as Code

AWS CloudFormation gives you an easy way to model a collection of related AWS and third-party resources, provision them quickly and consistently, and manage them throughout their lifecycles, by treating infrastructure as code.



  • Templates: YAML or JSON files that describe the resources you want.
  • Stacks: A collection of resources that you can manage as a single unit.
  • Consistent Deployment: Easily replicate your environment across different Regions.
CloudFormation Template (YAML)
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  MyS3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      AccessControl: Private

title: AWS Summary
content:
AWS Summary 🚀

Your Cloud Journey Begins

Congratulations! You have completed the expanded AWS curriculum. You now have a solid understanding of the core services and advanced concepts shaping the AWS Cloud.

  • Compute & Serverless: EC2, Lambda, and Auto Scaling.
  • Storage & Database: S3, RDS, and DynamoDB.
  • Networking & Security: VPC, IAM, and ELB.
  • Operations & Delivery: CloudWatch, CloudFront, and CloudFormation.

AWS is vast and ever-evolving. Your next step is to get hands-on experience and perhaps aim for a certification like the AWS Cloud Practitioner or Solutions Architect!
